figures.caption: Fig. 3. (a) Spin-resolved anomalous Hall conductance (𝜎ik,±) in spin-up (positive) and spin-down (negative) channels as a function of hypothetical 𝐸\ with rigid band assumption. The parameters are Δ = 2, 𝑈 = 3, and 𝜆 = 0.01, 0.03, and 0.05. (b) and (c) Distribution of Berry curvature (Ω) in spin-up and spin-down channels, respectively. (d) Band structure of a nanoribbon with width of 20.5 unit cells ended by A-C chains on the edges [see the inset in (e)]. (e) Local orbital weight for the states marked by the red and blue dots at 𝐸\ of the inset band structure which zooms in the region indicated by the dashed yellow rectangle in (d). The parameters for (b) to (e) are: Δ = 2, 𝑈 = 3, and 𝜆 = 0.05.

Fig. 1. (a) Structural model of two-dimensional Lieb lattice. ‘A’ stands for nonmagnetic sites, while ‘B’ and ‘C’ for magnetic sites with opposite spins. The parameters t1 and t2 indicate the hopping amplitudes between nearest and next-nearest neighbors. (b) The phase diagram of the electronic and magnetic features with respect to the values of Δ and 𝑈 without SOC. (c) Band structures with representative values of Δ and 𝑈 without SOC. The Fermi level is set to zero energy. (d) Number of electrons (solid lines) and spin moment (MS) (dashed-dotted lines) on B site as a function of 𝑈 with selected Δ.

Fig. 2. (a) Band structure with Δ = 2, 𝑈 = 3, and 𝜆 = 0.05. (b) Band gaps at 𝐸\ as a function of 𝜆. (c) and (d) Three-dimensional valence band and conduction band in spin-up and spin-down channels with the same parameters as (a).
